Overview Brain Probe Iteration Loop

Brain Probe: ComfyUI × TRIBE

Can a diffusion model — guided by a whiteboard/diagram prompt — produce images that TRIBE predicts will activate the face area (FFA) and motion cortex (MT+) more than the source frame it was given? We extracted peak-activation frames from the three highest-scoring @thecybersecrets videos, ran them through ComfyUI img2img (DreamShaper 8, denoise=0.55), then scored both source and generated image through TRIBE v2 on a local Quadro P4000.

Methodology
Each frame is looped into a 6-second silent video before TRIBE scoring — so absolute scores reflect a static image stimulus, not dynamic video. Negative values are expected for MT+ (no optical flow) and STS (no speech). The meaningful signal is the relative delta between source and generated. TRIBE predicts the average brain response across 25 fMRI training subjects.
Video A Router log analysis — phone screenshot mockup style 6% completion
Source frame (peak activation second)
ComfyUI img2img (DreamShaper 8, denoise=0.55)
TRIBE score FFA MT+ STS composite
original frame -0.067 -0.128 -0.157 -0.352
generated image +0.097 +0.081 -0.134 +0.044
delta +0.164+0.209+0.023 +0.397
Δ composite
+0.397
Cluttered screenshot → clean diagram. Source frame was the lowest-scoring still; generation pulled it hardest.
Video B Overkill Homelab — whiteboard network diagram 48% completion
Source frame (peak activation second)
ComfyUI img2img (DreamShaper 8, denoise=0.55)
TRIBE score FFA MT+ STS composite
original frame +0.220 +0.240 -0.109 +0.351
generated image +0.223 +0.259 -0.066 +0.416
delta +0.003+0.019+0.043 +0.065
Δ composite
+0.065
Already a crisp whiteboard diagram. Source frame was already high-scoring — almost no headroom for improvement.
Video C Dark floor/laptop opening shot — pre-content frame 61% completion
Source frame (peak activation second)
ComfyUI img2img (DreamShaper 8, denoise=0.55)
TRIBE score FFA MT+ STS composite
original frame +0.024 +0.026 -0.096 -0.046
generated image +0.278 +0.321 -0.094 +0.504
delta +0.254+0.295+0.001 +0.550
Δ composite
+0.550
Dark blurry floor shot → structured diagram workspace. Largest delta of all three: the model had the most to work with.
Summary — delta across all three videos
Video Source frame ΔFFA ΔMT+ ΔSTS Δcomposite
Video A Router log analysis — phone screenshot mockup style +0.164 +0.209 +0.023 +0.397
Video B Overkill Homelab — whiteboard network diagram +0.003 +0.019 +0.043 +0.065
Video C Dark floor/laptop opening shot — pre-content frame +0.254 +0.295 +0.001 +0.550
Finding: Delta is inversely proportional to how "readable" the source frame was. The clearest diagram (Video B) barely moved. The cluttered screenshot (A) and the dark blurry floor shot (C) got pulled hard toward the high-activation manifold — the largest delta (+0.550) came from the frame that had the most to gain. The whiteboard/diagram prompt acts as a gradient toward what TRIBE predicts face and motion cortex want to see.
